AIAutoCompactConfig.description: AIAutoCompactConfig contains auto-compact configuration for session history.

auto_include.description: Glob patterns for files to auto-include

exclude.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude

max_files.description: Maximum number of files to include (default: 100)

show_files.description: Show list of included files in UI (default: false)

AIContextSettings.description: AIContextSettings contains automatic context discovery configuration.

binary.description: CLI provider fields (for claude-code, codex-cli, copilot-cli, gemini-cli).

AIProviderConfig.description: AIProviderConfig contains configuration for a specific AI provider.

default_provider.description: Default provider. If empty or "auto", Atmos auto-detects an installed CLI provider (claude-code, codex-cli, copilot-cli, gemini-cli) on PATH, then falls back to anthropic

max_history_messages.description: Maximum conversation messages to keep in history (0 = unlimited)

max_history_tokens.description: Maximum tokens in conversation history (0 = unlimited). If both max_history_messages and max_history_tokens are set, whichever limit is hit first is applied

AISettings.description: AISettings contains configuration for AI assistant.

allowed_tools.description: Tool names this skill can use (empty = all tools)

AISkillConfig.description:
AISkillConfig contains configuration for a custom AI skill.
Skills follow the Agent Skills open standard (https://agentskills.io).

enabled.description:
Enabled gates whether atmos ai chat/ask/exec initialize the tool subsystem at all.
It does NOT gate the MCP server (`atmos mcp start`) — mcp.enabled is that command's
own, sufficient opt-in, and tools are always registered for MCP regardless of this flag.

allowed.description:
Allowed, when non-empty, restricts which tools are registered/exposed at all (by name
pattern) AND auto-approves them (no confirmation prompt). Empty/unset means all tools
are registered, subject to normal confirmation rules.

restricted.description: Restricted lists tools (already in the allowed set) that always require confirmation.

blocked.description: Blocked lists tools that are always denied outright.

identity.description: Atmos Auth identity for AWS credential injection.

region.description: Default AWS region (e.g., Security Hub aggregation region).

max_findings.description: Maximum findings per analysis run (0 = unlimited; controls AI costs when > 0).

account_map.description: Account ID → name for account-level findings.

AWSSecuritySettings.description: AWSSecuritySettings contains configuration for AWS security and compliance features.

AWSSecuritySources.description: AWSSecuritySources controls which AWS security services to query.

AWSSecurityTagMapping.description:
AWSSecurityTagMapping configures the tag keys used for finding-to-code mapping.
Only two tags are needed: one for the stack name and one for the component name.
These are configurable so organizations can use their own tagging standards.

AWSSettings.description: AWSSettings contains configuration for AWS-specific features.

auto_generate_files.description:
AutoGenerateFiles enables automatic generation of auxiliary configuration files
during Ansible operations when set to true.
Generated files are defined in the component's generate section.

Ansible.description: Ansible defines configuration for Ansible components.

experimental.description:
Experimental controls how experimental features are handled.
Values: "silence" (no output), "disable" (disabled), "warn" (default), "error" (exit).

docs.description: Deprecated: this was moved to top-level Atmos config

provision.description: Provision contains global defaults for provisioning.

key_delimiter.description:
KeyDelimiter enables nested key expansion in stack YAML files.
When set (e.g., "."), unquoted keys containing the delimiter are expanded
into nested map structures (e.g., "a.b: v" becomes "a: {b: v}").
Quoted keys are preserved as literal keys.
Empty string (default) disables expansion, preserving current behavior.

AtmosYAMLSettings.description: AtmosYAMLSettings controls YAML parsing behavior for stack files.

realm.description:
Realm provides credential isolation between different repositories or customer environments.
Different repositories with the same identity names will have isolated credentials.
If not set, defaults to a SHA256 hash of the CLI config path (first 8 characters).
Can also be set via ATMOS_AUTH_REALM environment variable (highest precedence).

AuthConfig.description: AuthConfig defines the authentication configuration structure.

isolated.description: Enable isolated browser sessions per identity.

AuthConsoleConfig.description: AuthConsoleConfig defines global console behavior settings under auth.console.

CIAnnotationsConfig.description:
CIAnnotationsConfig configures inline annotations for scanner-hook findings.
GitHub: workflow `::error`/`::warning` commands shown inline on the PR diff.
This is the "non-CodeQL" path — it needs no GitHub Advanced Security.

Enabled is *bool so callers can distinguish "unset" (nil, default applies)
from explicit false. Default is true when omitted; effective only when the
global ci.enabled is true.

enabled.description: Enabled is the master switch for the CI cache (env: ATMOS_CI_CACHE_ENABLED).

auto.description:
Auto controls automatic behavior: "off" (default), "restore", "save", or "both".
"restore" restores on startup; "save" saves on exit; "both" does both. The
explicit `atmos ci cache` subcommands are always available regardless.

root.description:
Root overrides the well-known cache directory that is archived. Defaults
to the Atmos XDG cache directory (~/.cache/atmos), of which the toolchain
is a sub-path. Honors ATMOS_XDG_CACHE_HOME / XDG_CACHE_HOME when unset.

paths.description:
Paths are root-relative subpaths to include in the cache. Empty means the
entire root is cached.

key.description:
Key is the cache key. Supports Go templates with {{.OS}}, {{.Arch}} and a
hashFiles function. Defaults to a key derived from the toolchain lockfile
hash plus OS/arch when unset.

restore_keys.description:
RestoreKeys are prefix fallbacks tried (in order) when the exact key is
absent, mirroring actions/cache restore-keys.

compression.description:
Compression selects the archive compression. Currently only "gzip" (the
default) is supported.

CICacheConfig.description:
CICacheConfig configures the CI build cache, which restores a well-known
cache directory (the toolchain install path and anything else under the XDG
cache root) at Atmos startup and saves it at exit, using the active CI
provider's cache store (e.g. the GitHub Actions cache). All operations are
no-ops when no cache-capable CI provider is detected (i.e. outside CI).

A fixed set of Atmos's own auth session-cache subdirectories (AWS SSO
tokens, Azure device-code tokens, AWS webflow refresh tokens, provisioned
identity metadata) is always excluded from the cache, regardless of Paths.
See docs/prd/native-ci/framework/ci-cache.md#default-excluded-auth-paths.

enabled.description:
Enabled controls whether commit status checks are created.
When nil (not set), defaults to false (disabled) as checks require extra permissions.
When explicitly set to true, checks are enabled.

CIChecksConfig.description:
CIChecksConfig configures CI commit status checks.
GitHub: Commit Status API, GitLab: Commit Status API.

component.description: Component controls the component-level status (e.g., atmos/plan/stack/component).

add.description:
Add controls the per-operation add status (e.g., atmos/plan/stack/component/add).
Only created when resources to add > 0.

change.description:
Change controls the per-operation change status (e.g., atmos/plan/stack/component/change).
Only created when resources to change > 0.

destroy.description:
Destroy controls the per-operation destroy status (e.g., atmos/plan/stack/component/destroy).
Only created when resources to destroy > 0.

CIChecksStatusesConfig.description:
CIChecksStatusesConfig configures which per-operation commit statuses are created.
All fields default to true when nil (not set).

CICommentsConfig.description:
CICommentsConfig configures PR/MR comment integration.
GitHub: PR comments, GitLab: MR notes.

Enabled is *bool so callers can distinguish "unset" (nil, default applies)
from explicit false. Default is true when omitted; see `isCommentsEnabled`.

allow_unsafe_fork_execution.description:
AllowUnsafeForkExecution opts out of the fork-checkout safety gate that
refuses to clone untrusted fork content under pull_request_target and
workflow_run events. Leave false unless a fork-facing workflow has a
documented reason to bypass it. See
docs/prd/native-ci/framework/fork-pr-trust-gate.md.

CIConfig.description:
CIConfig contains CI/CD integration configuration.
Uses provider-agnostic naming to support GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and other providers.

CIGroupsConfig.description:
CIGroupsConfig configures collapsible CI log groups. On GitHub Actions this
emits the `::group::<name>` / `::endgroup::` workflow commands that fold a
region of the run log into a named, expandable section.

Mode selects the grouping granularity (mutually exclusive — CI providers do
not support nested groups):

  - "auto" (default when omitted): the finest granularity that applies to
    each command — one group per workflow/custom-command step, and one group
    per phase (init/plan/apply) of a terraform/helmfile/packer invocation.
  - "invocation": one group around each whole top-level `atmos <command>`
    run; finer step/phase grouping is suppressed.
  - "off": no grouping.

Effective only when the global ci.enabled is true and a grouping-capable CI
provider is detected.

enabled.description:
Enabled controls whether output variables are written.
When nil (not set), defaults to true (enabled).
When explicitly set to false, output is disabled.

CIOutputConfig.description:
CIOutputConfig configures CI output variables for downstream jobs.
GitHub: $GITHUB_OUTPUT, GitLab: dotenv artifacts.

CIResultsConfig.description:
CIResultsConfig configures uploading scanner-hook findings (SARIF) to the
CI provider's security-findings store. GitHub: Code Scanning / the Security
tab, which requires GitHub Advanced Security on private repos and a token
with the `security_events` write scope.

Enabled is *bool so callers can distinguish "unset" (nil, default applies)
from explicit false. Default is false when omitted (opt-in, since it has
side effects and extra requirements); effective only when ci.enabled is true.

enabled.description:
Enabled controls whether job summaries are written.
When nil (not set), defaults to true (enabled).
When explicitly set to false, summaries are disabled.

CISummaryConfig.description:
CISummaryConfig configures CI job summary output.
GitHub: $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY, GitLab: job artifacts.

base_path.description:
BasePath is the base directory for custom templates.
Relative paths are resolved from atmos.yaml location.

terraform.description:
Terraform contains template overrides for terraform commands.
Keys are command names (e.g., "plan", "apply"), values are template file paths.

helm.description:
Helm contains template overrides for native Helm commands.
Keys are command names (e.g., "diff", "apply"), values are template file paths.

helmfile.description:
Helmfile contains template overrides for helmfile commands.
Keys are command names (e.g., "diff", "apply"), values are template file paths.

CITemplatesConfig.description: CITemplatesConfig configures CI summary templates per component type.

CastConfig.description: CastConfig configures Atmos cast recording behavior.

CastDefaults.description: CastDefaults configures defaults applied to child steps inside a cast step.

CastRecordingConfig.description: CastRecordingConfig configures automatic cast recording options.

CastRecordingDefaults.description: CastRecordingDefaults configures defaults for the cast recording itself.

CastSimulateDefaults.description: CastSimulateDefaults configures defaults for direct child type: simulate steps.

dependencies.description: Dependencies specifies external tool dependencies that must be installed before running this command.

steps.description:
Steps supports both simple string syntax and structured syntax.
Simple: ["echo hello", "echo world"]
Structured: [{name: step1, command: echo hello, timeout: 30s}]
Mixed: Both formats can be used in the same list.

Command.description: Command defines a custom CLI command.

type.description:
Type specifies the semantic type of this argument: "component" or "stack".
When set, the argument value is used to resolve component configuration.

CommandArgument.description: CommandArgument defines a positional argument for a custom command.

type.description: Type is the component type name (e.g., "script", "ansible", "manifest").

base_path.description:
BasePath is the base directory for components of this type.
Defaults to "components/<type>" if not specified.

CommandComponent.description:
CommandComponent defines a custom component type for a command.
When specified, the command can access component configuration via {{ .Component.* }} templates.

CommandComponentConfig.description: CommandComponentConfig defines component configuration for a custom command (legacy).

CommandEnv.description: CommandEnv defines an environment variable for a custom command.

semantic_type.description:
SemanticType specifies the semantic type of this flag: "component" or "stack".
When set, the flag value is used to resolve component configuration.

CommandFlag.description: CommandFlag defines a flag for a custom command.

component.description:
Component instance name (canonical). This is the name under
components.<kind>.<name>, not the Terraform module path.

name.description:
Name is an input-side alias for Component introduced in the v2 surface.
It is normalized into Component by Dependencies.Normalize. Code should
always read from Component, never from Name.

stack.description: Stack name (optional, defaults to current stack). Supports Go templates.

kind.description:
Kind specifies the dependency type: terraform, helmfile, packer, plugin
type, or — in the legacy inline shape — file/folder. Defaults to the
declaring component's type for component dependencies.

path.description:
Path for file or folder dependencies (legacy inline shape). For new
configurations, prefer the sibling keys `dependencies.files` /
`dependencies.folders`.

ComponentDependency.description:
ComponentDependency represents a single dependency entry. It supports two
surfaces simultaneously:

  - The canonical (recommended) shape uses `name` for the component instance
    name and an optional `kind` for cross-type dependencies (helmfile, packer,
    plugin). Path-based dependencies should be declared using the sibling
    keys `dependencies.files` / `dependencies.folders` rather than entries
    here.

  - The legacy shape uses `component` (instead of `name`) and supports
    `kind: file` / `kind: folder` with `path:` to declare path-based
    dependencies inline. This shape continues to parse for backward
    compatibility with stack configurations from Atmos < v1.211.0.

Use the helpers IsFileDependency / IsFolderDependency / IsComponentDependency
to discriminate; do not branch on `Kind` directly.

terraform.description: Built-in component types (legacy - will migrate to plugin model in future phases).

description.description: Description is a human-readable summary of the composition.

services.description: Services is the closed set of service names that may claim membership.

Composition.description:
Composition declares a named multi-service system. Components join a
composition via their first-class `composition:` field. The `Services` list is
a closed contract for membership (declaring membership in an unlisted service
is an error) but open for fulfillment (a declared service with no component in
a given stack is allowed).

Config.description: Config holds the configuration for the profiler.

name.description: Name is the name of the configuration or profile.

version.description: Version is the semantic version of the configuration.

tags.description: Tags are labels for filtering and organization.

deprecated.description: Deprecated indicates if this configuration should no longer be used.

ConfigMetadata.description: ConfigMetadata contains metadata about a configuration file or profile.

ConsoleConfig.description: ConsoleConfig defines web console access configuration for providers.

ContainerConfig.description:
ContainerConfig is the top-level `container:` namespace for global container
behavior. It is the forward-compatible home for settings shared by the
`type: container` workflow steps and the upcoming `container` component kind /
`atmos container` command.

ContainerMount.description: ContainerMount represents a volume mount for container steps.

ContainerPort.description: ContainerPort represents a port mapping for container steps.

provider.description:
Provider selects the container runtime: "auto" | "docker" | "podman" | "".
"auto" and "" auto-detect Docker, then Podman. A global default for the
per-step `provider:` field.

auto_start.description:
AutoStart lets Atmos auto-init/start the Podman machine when no running runtime
is found, instead of failing. Enabled by default; a global default for the per-step
`runtime_auto_start:` field; also settable via ATMOS_CONTAINER_RUNTIME_AUTO_START.

host.description:
Host grants the container access to the host container runtime (Docker-out-of-Docker):
Atmos mounts the runtime socket, runs the container as root, relabels it for SELinux,
and sets DOCKER_HOST so the container can launch and manage sibling containers. Opt-in
and effectively host-root; independent of `privileged` (kernel caps). On rootless
podman the socket is unreachable in-container — use Docker or `podman machine set --rootful`.

ContainerRuntimeConfig.description: ContainerRuntimeConfig configures the container runtime provider (auto/docker/podman).

tools.description: Tools maps tool names to version constraints (e.g., "terraform": "1.5.0" or "latest").

components.description:
Components lists component dependencies that must be applied before this component.
Uses list format with always-append merge behavior (child lists extend parent lists).
After Normalize, this slice also contains synthetic entries for any sibling
`files` / `folders` declarations (Kind set to "file" / "folder").

files.description:
Files lists file paths whose changes mark the declaring component as
affected by `atmos describe affected`. Each entry may be a plain string
(the file path) or, in the future, an object with additional options.

folders.description:
Folders lists folder paths whose changes mark the declaring component as
affected by `atmos describe affected`. Each entry may be a plain string
(the folder path) or, in the future, an object with additional options.

Dependencies.description:
Dependencies declares required tools and component dependencies.

The canonical (v2) input surface has four sibling keys: `tools`,
`components`, `files`, `folders`. The legacy v1 surface (which still
parses) puts file/folder entries inside `components[]` discriminated by
`kind: file` / `kind: folder`. After unmarshaling from a stack manifest,
callers MUST invoke Normalize so both surfaces are reconciled into a
single internal representation: `Components` ends up holding every
dependency entry (component, file, and folder kinds), which is what the
downstream describe-affected / describe-dependents pipelines consume.

error_mode.description:
ErrorMode is the project-wide default for the `describe` commands' (`describe stacks`,
`describe affected`, `describe dependents`) `--error-mode` flag: how to handle a
recoverable per-value YAML function error (e.g. a Terraform backend that has not been
provisioned yet). Values: "strict", "warn" (default), "silent". An explicit --error-mode
flag or ATMOS_DESCRIBE_ERROR_MODE env var overrides this.

Describe.description: Describe contains configuration for the describe command.

sections.description:
Sections is the authoritative list of top-level component sections compared
when computing affected components. When set, it REPLACES the built-in defaults.
When empty, the built-in defaults are used. `metadata` and `settings` are always
evaluated via dedicated logic and are not controlled by this list.

DescribeAffected.description: DescribeAffected contains configuration for the `atmos describe affected` command.

DescribeSettings.description: DescribeSettings contains settings for the describe command output.

Diagnostics.description: Diagnostics contains machine-readable diagnostic event stream settings.

ECRRegistry.description: ECRRegistry represents an ECR registry configuration for aws/ecr integrations.

name.description: Name is the EKS cluster name (required).

region.description: Region is the AWS region where the cluster is located (required).

alias.description: Alias is the context name in kubeconfig (optional, defaults to cluster ARN).

EKSCluster.description: EKSCluster represents an EKS cluster configuration for aws/eks integrations.

exclude.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude

EnvironmentVariable.description: EnvironmentVariable defines an environment variable with preserved case sensitivity.

ErrorFormatConfig.description: ErrorFormatConfig contains formatting options for errors.

ErrorsConfig.description: ErrorsConfig contains configuration for error handling.

GitAuthConfig.description:
GitAuthConfig selects the Atmos Auth identity for a repository.
Integrations are never referenced by name; they attach via identity links.

GitAuthorConfig.description: GitAuthorConfig is the commit author/committer identity.

depth.description: Depth is the shallow-clone depth; 0 means full history (default).

filter.description: Filter is an optional partial-clone filter spec (e.g. "blob:none").

single_branch.description: SingleBranch limits the clone to the configured branch.

submodules.description: Submodules enables submodule initialization. Default: false.

GitCloneConfig.description: GitCloneConfig controls clone behavior for a managed repository.

signing.description: Signing mode: "auto" (default; Git config decides), "always" (-S), or "never" (--no-gpg-sign).

author.description:
Author overrides the commit author/committer identity (required in CI
where no user.name/user.email is configured).

GitCommitConfig.description: GitCommitConfig controls commit signing and author identity.

repositories.description: Repositories maps user-defined logical names to managed Git repositories.

hooks.description:
Hooks maps local Git hook names (pre-commit, commit-msg, ...) to commands
executed by `atmos git hooks run` via generated .git/hooks shims.

list.description: List configures `atmos git list` output (columns, format).

GitHookEntry.description: GitHookEntry configures a local Git hook executed via `atmos git hooks run`.

from.description:
From seeds the new repository from another repository's content. It acts
as the default for the `--from` flag; the flag overrides it when set.

keep_history.description:
KeepHistory preserves the source's full history and keeps the source
reachable as the 'upstream' remote. Only valid together with From.
The `--keep-history` flag also enables it.

GitInitConfig.description: GitInitConfig sets defaults for `atmos git init` on a managed repository.

retries.description:
Retries bounds the pull-ff/re-push loop on non-fast-forward rejection.
nil means the default (3). 0 disables retries.

provider.description: Provider selects the execution backend. Default and only v1 value: "cli".

uri.description: URI is the remote repository URI. Credentials must never be embedded.

branch.description: Branch to check out; empty means the remote default branch.

workdir.description: Workdir overrides the automatic XDG workdir location.

clone.description: Clone controls clone depth and related options.

auth.description: Auth selects the Atmos Auth identity used for Git operations on this repository.

commit.description: Commit controls commit signing and author identity.

init.description: Init sets defaults for `atmos git init` on this repository.

GitRepository.description: GitRepository describes a managed Git repository under git.repositories.<name>.

git_config_mode.description:
GitConfigMode controls how minted tokens reach the child process git config:
"env" (inline GIT_CONFIG_KEY_n/VALUE_n) or "file" (write a 0600 gitconfig and emit include.path).
Defaults to "env" when unset.

revoke_on_exit.description: RevokeOnExit controls command-end auto-revocation of minted tokens (in CI). Defaults to true when unset.

GitSTSSettings.description:
GitSTSSettings contains global defaults for the github/sts auth integration.
Per-integration spec fields override these.

GithubOIDCSettings.description: GithubOIDCSettings contains GitHub OIDC token configuration.

status.description: Status lists acceptable HTTP status codes. When set, the response status must be in this list.

response.description:
Response lists regular expressions; when set, the response body must match at least one.
Patterns may be written as /.../ literals (surrounding slashes are stripped) or bare regex strings.

HTTPExpect.description:
HTTPExpect defines success criteria for an http step.
When unset, any 2xx response is considered a success.

auto_generate_files.description:
AutoGenerateFiles enables automatic generation of auxiliary configuration files
during Helm operations when set to true.
Generated files are defined in the component's generate section.

source.description: Source holds global source configuration defaults for JIT-vendored components.

plugins.description:
Plugins lists Helm CLI plugins (e.g. helm-diff, helm-secrets) to install
into the managed HELM_PLUGINS directory. Each entry is a compact spec: a
short alias (e.g. "diff@3.9.x"), an "owner/repo@version", or a full plugin
URL. Native Helm SDK operations do not use plugins; these apply only to
helm-binary passthrough. Type-level defaults inherit down to instances via
normal stack deep-merge.

repositories.description:
Repositories lists reusable Helm chart repositories available to native
Helm components. Component-level repositories override entries by name.

Helm.description:
Helm defines configuration for native Helm components.
Helm components render and deploy Helm charts (local, remote-repo, or OCI)
using the Helm Go SDK, and can deliver rendered manifests to provision targets.

password.description: #nosec G117 -- Helm repository credentials are part of user configuration.

HelmRepository.description: HelmRepository describes an HTTP Helm chart repository.

cluster_name_pattern.description: Deprecated: use ClusterNameTemplate with Go template syntax.

auto_generate_files.description:
AutoGenerateFiles enables automatic generation of auxiliary configuration files
during Helmfile operations when set to true.
Generated files are defined in the component's generate section.

source.description: Source holds global source configuration defaults for JIT-vendored components.

plugins.description:
Plugins lists Helm CLI plugins (e.g. helm-diff, helm-secrets) required by
helmfile components. Each entry is a compact spec: a short alias
(e.g. "diff@3.9.x"), an "owner/repo@version", or a full plugin URL.
Atmos installs them into a managed HELM_PLUGINS directory before invoking
the helmfile binary. Type-level defaults declared here inherit down to
component instances via normal stack deep-merge.

required.description:
Required marks this identity for automatic authentication without prompting.
Multiple identities can be required. Required identities that are not default
are authenticated as secondary — their profiles are written to the shared
credentials file for multi-account Terraform components.

provider.description: Provider name for direct provider association (for provisioned identities).

principal.description: Principal information (role name, account, etc.). For AWS permission sets: {name: string, account: {name: string, id: string}}.

emulator.description:
Emulator references an emulator component by name (resolved against the stack
the command runs in). Used by emulator identity kinds (kind: <target>/emulator)
to harvest the running emulator's connection profile (SDK env vars or kubeconfig).

tags.description: Tags are user-defined categorical labels for filtering (atmos auth list/login --tags).

Identity.description: Identity defines an authentication identity configuration.

IdentityVia.description: IdentityVia defines how an identity connects to a provider or other identity.

ttl.description:
TTL is the default cache duration for the cloned source repos of remote (git)
imports, applied when a per-import `ttl` is not set. See StackImport.TTL for the
caching semantics and accepted formats.

ImportsSettings.description: ImportsSettings holds global defaults applied when processing stack imports.

Integration.description:
Integration defines a client-only credential materialization (e.g., ECR, EKS).
Integrations derive credentials from identities for service-specific access.

auto_provision.description: Whether to auto-provision on identity login. Defaults to true.

registry.description: Single ECR registry for aws/ecr integrations.

revoke_on_exit.description: Auto-revoke minted tokens at command-end. Overrides settings.pro default.

token_env.description: Env var name to export the raw minted token under: a literal (e.g. "GH_TOKEN") or a Go template over .owner/.host (e.g. "GH_TOKEN_{{ .owner }}"). Empty defaults to "ATMOS_PRO_GITHUB_TOKEN" so the token bridges to gh/REST and Atmos's in-process git detector.

IntegrationSpec.description: IntegrationSpec defines the spec configuration for integrations.

IntegrationVia.description:
IntegrationVia defines how an integration connects to an identity or provider.
Exactly one of Identity or Provider must be set. Provider binding is useful when
the provider itself yields usable credentials (e.g., atmos/pro session JWT) and a
passthrough identity would otherwise be redundant.

KeyringConfig.description: KeyringConfig defines keyring backend configuration for credential storage.

path.description: Path is a custom kubeconfig file path (optional, defaults to XDG path).

mode.description:
Mode is the file permission mode as an octal string (optional, defaults to "0600").
Parsed via strconv.ParseUint(mode, 8, 32) at config-load time.

update.description: Update determines how to handle existing kubeconfig: "merge" (default), "replace", or "error".

provider.description: Provider selects the SDK-backed renderer/loader. Supported values are kubectl and kustomize.

auto_generate_files.description:
AutoGenerateFiles enables automatic generation of auxiliary configuration files
during Kubernetes operations when set to true.
Generated files are defined in the component's generate section.

source.description: Source holds global source configuration defaults for JIT-vendored components.

Kubernetes.description: Kubernetes defines configuration for Kubernetes components.

LSPServer.description: LSPServer contains configuration for a single LSP server.

LSPSettings.description: LSPSettings contains configuration for Language Server Protocol integration.

format.description:
Format specifies the output format (table, json, csv)
If empty, defaults to table format

enabled.description: Enabled controls whether automatic server routing is active (default: true).

MCPRoutingConfig.description:
MCPRoutingConfig configures the two-pass routing that selects which MCP servers
are relevant to a user's question before starting them.
Routing uses the same AI provider and model configured under ai.default_provider.

command.description: Standard MCP server fields (compatible with mcpServers JSON format).

identity.description: Identity is the Atmos Auth identity (from the auth section) for credential injection.

cwd.description:
Cwd is the working directory a stdio server subprocess should be launched from.
Used for user-scoped (global) client installs, where the client config isn't
colocated with the Atmos project, so the server needs an explicit cwd.

MCPServerConfig.description:
MCPServerConfig represents an external MCP server configured in atmos.yaml
under mcp.servers. The core fields (command, args, env) follow the standard
MCP server configuration format used by Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI.
Atmos-specific extensions (description, auto_start, timeout, identity)
provide additional functionality.

MCPSettings.description:
MCPSettings contains configuration for the MCP (Model Context Protocol) server
and external MCP server connections.

MaskSettings.description: MaskSettings contains configuration for sensitive data masking.

auto_generate_files.description:
AutoGenerateFiles enables automatic generation of auxiliary configuration files
during Packer operations when set to true.
Generated files are defined in the component's generate section.

source.description: Source holds global source configuration defaults for JIT-vendored components.

ParallelFailConfig.description: ParallelFailConfig configures failure behavior for parallel and matrix steps.

PlanfileStoreSpec.description: PlanfileStoreSpec defines a planfile storage backend.

verify.description:
Verify controls drift verification of the stored planfile on `deploy`.
One of: fail | warn | off. Empty means unset (defaults to fail under CI
when planfile storage is configured, otherwise off).

required.description:
Required controls whether a stored planfile must exist to verify against on
`deploy` (distinct from Verify, which governs the drift comparison when a
stored plan exists). A nil pointer means unset: required tracks verify
strictness (true when verification resolves to fail, e.g. under CI with
storage configured), so a fail-by-default deploy fails loudly rather than
silently applying an unverified fresh plan. true requires a stored plan;
false applies a fresh plan when none is found. It only applies when
verification is active (verify is not off).

PlanfilesConfig.description: PlanfilesConfig contains configuration for planfile storage backends.

git_sts.description: GitSTS holds global defaults for the github/sts auth integration.

ProSettings.description: ProSettings contains Atmos Pro integration configuration.

base_path.description:
BasePath is the custom directory for profile storage.
If relative, resolved from atmos.yaml directory.
If absolute, used as-is.

default.description:
Default is the name of a profile loaded automatically when neither the
--profile flag nor the ATMOS_PROFILE environment variable is set.
The default is treated as implicit: an explicit --profile or ATMOS_PROFILE
always wins, and it does not suppress the interactive identity fallback.

ProfilesConfig.description: ProfilesConfig defines configuration for the profiles system.

browser_type.description: Browser engine type: chromium, firefox, webkit, chrome, msedge, etc.

tags.description: Tags are user-defined categorical labels for filtering (atmos auth list/logout --tags).

Provider.description: Provider defines an authentication provider configuration.

default.description: Default is the name of the target used by apply/deploy when no --target is given.

targets.description: Targets maps target names to delivery destinations for rendered artifacts.

ProvisionSettings.description: ProvisionSettings contains global defaults for provisioning.

repository.description: Repository references a top-level git.repositories.<name> (git kind).

path.description: Path is the destination path inside the deployment repository (git kind, supports templates).

auth.description: Auth selects the Atmos Auth identity used for the delivery (overrides the repository default).

commit.description: Commit controls the commit message and signing for the delivery (git kind).

pull_request.description: PullRequest configures pull-request publishing (git kind; not yet supported by the cli provider).

ProvisionTarget.description:
ProvisionTarget is a delivery destination for a rendered artifact, selected by
its kind (e.g. "git", "kubernetes"). Kind-specific fields below are interpreted
by the registered target provisioner for that kind.

ProvisionTargetAuth.description: ProvisionTargetAuth selects the Atmos Auth identity for a delivery.

message.description: Message is the commit message (supports templates).

ProvisionTargetCommit.description: ProvisionTargetCommit controls commit message and signing for a git delivery.

enabled.description: Enabled requests pull-request publishing (not yet supported by the cli provider).

ProvisionTargetPullRequest.description: ProvisionTargetPullRequest configures pull-request publishing for a git delivery.

enabled.description: Enabled sets the default enabled state for workdir provisioning.

ttl.description:
TTL is the default time-to-live for workdirs (e.g., "7d", "24h", "weekly").
Workdirs not accessed within this duration can be cleaned up.

ProvisionWorkdirSettings.description: ProvisionWorkdirSettings contains default settings for workdir provisioning.

conditions.description:
Conditions is a list of regex patterns matched against captured subprocess output
to decide whether an error is recoverable and the command should be retried.
When empty or nil, no pattern-based retry is performed (all failures are terminal).
Patterns may be wrapped in optional /.../ delimiters for readability.

RetryConfig.description:
RetryConfig represents the retry configuration.
Pointer types are used to distinguish between "not specified" (nil) and "explicitly set to zero".
- nil means the feature is disabled or unlimited (e.g., nil MaxAttempts = unlimited retries).
- Explicitly setting a value to zero is invalid and will cause a validation error.

identity.description:
Identity optionally names an auth identity (resolved via pkg/auth) used by KMS-backed
SOPS kinds (sops/aws-kms, sops/gcp-kms). Local-key kinds (sops/age, sops/gpg) ignore it.

spec.description: Spec carries provider-specific options (e.g. file, age_recipients, kms arn).

SecretProviderConfig.description: SecretProviderConfig configures a single non-store secret provider (track 2).

providers.description:
Providers maps a provider name to its configuration. These are non-store backends
(e.g. SOPS) that do not fit the key-value store interface.

SecretsConfig.description:
SecretsConfig is the top-level `secrets:` block in atmos.yaml. It holds non-store
secret backends (track 2), such as SOPS encrypted files. Store-backed secret backends
(track 1) are configured as regular `stores:` entries with `secret: true` instead.

SentryConfig.description: SentryConfig contains Sentry error reporting configuration.

SessionConfig.description: SessionConfig defines session configuration for providers.

header.description: Header auto-displays workflow description as styled header before first step.

flags.description: Flags displays workflow-level flag values under header (e.g., "stack: dev").

command.description: Command shows step command before execution (with $ prefix).

progress.description: Progress shows progress bar pinned to bottom (Docker-build style, TTY only).

labels.description: Labels shows step boundary labels and completion/failure footers.

ShowConfig.description:
ShowConfig configures automatic display features for workflows.
All fields use *bool to enable tri-state logic: nil (inherit), true, false.
This allows step-level settings to override workflow-level defaults via deep merge.

SimulatePrompt.description: SimulatePrompt configures the rendered prompt for cast simulation steps.

ttl.description:
TTL is the default cache duration for JIT-vendored sources.
Applied to all components unless overridden per-component.
If not set, cached sources are reused indefinitely.

SourceSettings.description: SourceSettings holds global source configuration defaults for JIT-vendored components.

metadata.description:
Metadata controls whether metadata fields are inherited from base components.
When true (default), all metadata fields except 'inherits' are inherited.
When false, metadata is per-component only (legacy behavior).

source.description:
Source controls whether source configuration is inherited from base components.
When true (default), source configuration is inherited and deep merged.
When false, source is per-component only.

StacksInherit.description: StacksInherit controls inheritance behavior for stack processing.

type.description: Type is the legacy backend selector (e.g. "aws-ssm-parameter-store").

kind.description:
Kind is the new cloud/thing backend selector (e.g. "aws/ssm"); when set it takes
precedence over Type. The registry maps legacy Type to Kind for backward compatibility.

secret.description:
Secret marks this store as a secret backend (subsystem membership). A secret store
is the only backend the !secret function and the `atmos secret` CLI resolve from, and
`!store` against it is an error ("use !secret"). Secret stores always write the
sensitive variant at rest (e.g. SSM SecureString).

command.description: Command is the command to execute.

script.description: Script is the inline script body for TaskTypeScript.

interpreter.description: Interpreter is the executable used to run Script for TaskTypeScript.

type.description: Type specifies the step type: shell, script, atmos, exec, cast, simulate, workdir, or another registered step kind. Defaults to shell.

timeout.description: Timeout specifies the maximum duration for the task. Zero means no timeout.

stack.description: Stack specifies the stack to use for atmos commands.

working_directory.description: WorkingDirectory specifies the working directory for the command.

retry.description: Retry specifies retry configuration for failed tasks.

identity.description: Identity specifies the authentication identity to use.

needs.description: Needs lists sibling task names that must complete before this task can run.

anyOf.description: Predicate (always, never, ci, local, success, failure) or CEL expression.

when.description: When controls whether the task runs.

interactive.description: Interactive attaches host stdin to the step and lets the step handle Ctrl-C (like docker -i).

tty.description: Tty allocates a pseudo-terminal for the step (like docker -t). Combine with interactive for full terminal sessions.

source.description: Source: workdir provisioning (string or source map), or the directory/file to archive (archive step type, string only).

reset.description: Reset the target path before provisioning.

border.description: Border style: none, hidden, normal, rounded, thick, double.

padding.description: Padding: "1" or "1 2" or "1 2 1 2" (top, right, bottom, left).

margin.description: Margin: "1" or "1 2" or "1 2 1 2" (top, right, bottom, left).

url.description: HTTP step type fields (type: http; also accepts the alias type: webhook).

method.description: HTTP method/verb: GET (default), POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS.

body.description: Raw request body (supports templates); mutually exclusive with form.

form.description: Form/JSON body params; mutually exclusive with body.

mode.description: Cast step and session action fields.

jitter.description: Deterministic typing delay variance for simulated typed cast steps.

cursor.description: Show a simulated cursor for cast simulate steps.

action.description:
Container step fields.

Action selects the container verb; its parameters are supplied under the single
`with:` key. Build/Push/Run/Inspect are populated from `with:` by UnmarshalYAML
based on Action, so they carry no YAML key (see decodeContainerWith).

Only cross-cutting execution modifiers stay top-level (provider, runtime_auto_start,
container). All action parameters — image, command, ports, mounts, healthcheck, etc. —
live under `with:` (decoded into Build/Run/Push/Inspect).

provider.description: auto, docker, podman, or empty for auto-detect.

container.description: Workflow container override or false to run on host.

format.description:
Archive step fields (type: archive). Action reuses the container step's
Action field (create | extract | update | replace); Source reuses the
workdir step's Source field (archive requires it to be a string path).

destination.description: Pack: archive file to write. Extract: directory to extract into.

subpath.description: Pack: nest source content under this path inside the archive. Extract: only extract this path, prefix stripped.

exclude.description: Glob(s); drop matching files, evaluated before include.

mtime.description:
Mtime controls the modification-time metadata stamped into each archive entry
(not the source files on disk, not the archive file's own OS-level mtime):
filesystem (default, same as omitting the field) | epoch (one shared timestamp
for the whole archive) | git (per-entry timestamps). epoch/git also normalize
permission bits. See docs/prd/archive-step.md.

tools.description:
Require step type fields (type: require; also accepts the alias type: assert).
The step is a read-only preconditions gate: it never mutates PATH or the environment.

hint.description: Extra remediation note appended to the failure error (supports templates).

outputs.description: Outputs declares named outputs derived from the step result.

show.description: Show configuration for this step (overrides workflow-level show settings).

Task.description:
Task represents a unit of work that can be executed.
This type unifies workflow steps and custom command steps,
supporting both simple string syntax and structured syntax.

anyOf.description: Shell command shorthand for a step.

Tasks.description:
Tasks is a slice of Task that supports flexible YAML unmarshaling.
It can parse both simple string syntax and structured syntax:

Simple syntax:

	steps:
	  - "echo hello"
	  - "echo world"

Structured syntax:

	steps:
	  - name: greet
	    command: echo hello
	    timeout: 30s

Mixed syntax:

	steps:
	  - "echo simple"
	  - name: complex
	    command: echo structured
	    timeout: 5m

TelemetrySettings.description: TelemetrySettings contains configuration for telemetry collection.

ignore_missing_template_values.description:
IgnoreMissingTemplateValues is the global default for ignoring missing template values.
When true, template processing will not fail if a template variable is missing.
This can be overridden per-import using the import's own ignore_missing_template_values setting.

no_color.description: Deprecated in config, use Color instead

auto_generate_files.description:
AutoGenerateFiles enables automatic generation of auxiliary configuration files
(e.g., .tf, .json, .yaml) during Terraform operations when set to true.
Generated files are defined in the component's generate section.

plugin_cache.description:
PluginCache enables automatic Terraform provider plugin caching.
When true, Atmos sets TF_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR to XDG cache or PluginCacheDir.
Default: true.

plugin_cache_dir.description:
PluginCacheDir is an optional custom path for the plugin cache.
If empty and PluginCache is true, uses XDG cache: ~/.cache/atmos/terraform/plugins.

auto_provision_workdir_for_outputs.description:
AutoProvisionWorkdirForOutputs controls whether Atmos automatically provisions
JIT working directories before running terraform output.
When true (default), output fetching transparently provisions the workdir
if provision.workdir.enabled is set on the referenced component.

source.description: Source holds global source configuration defaults for JIT-vendored components.

ci.description:
CI holds terraform-specific CI configuration (e.g., exit code mapping).
Only active when the global ci.enabled is true.

workspace.description: Workspace holds workspace-related configuration for backend key generation.

rc.description:
RC holds Terraform CLI configuration (.terraformrc) that Atmos renders and
exposes to the terraform/tofu subprocess via TF_CLI_CONFIG_FILE. Disabled by default.

cache.description:
Cache holds the Terraform registry cache configuration (provider + module
caching via an ephemeral network-mirror proxy). Disabled by default.

platforms.description:
Platforms lists the target platforms a project builds for, as <os>_<arch>
(e.g. linux_amd64, darwin_arm64). It is consumed by `atmos terraform cache
mirror` (eager multi-platform pre-seeding) and by the automatic post-init
`providers lock` that keeps .terraform.lock.hcl complete across platforms.
Empty defaults to the current host platform.

exit_codes.description:
ExitCodes maps terraform exit codes to success/failure for CI runners.
When a code maps to true, Atmos exits 0 (success). When false or unmapped,
the original exit code is preserved. Only active when global ci.enabled is true.

TerraformCI.description: TerraformCI holds CI-specific configuration for terraform components.

type.description: Type is the storage backend: filesystem (default, maps to artifact local/dir).

TerraformCacheBackend.description: TerraformCacheBackend selects and configures the cache storage backend.

enabled.description: Enabled turns on the registry cache. Defaults to false.

location.description:
Location is the cache root. Defaults to the XDG cache directory
(~/.cache/atmos or $XDG_CACHE_HOME/atmos) under terraform/registry.

backend.description: Backend selects the storage backend. filesystem (default) in V1.

metadata_ttl.description: MetadataTTL is the time-to-live for registry metadata. Defaults to 24h.

stale_while_revalidate.description:
StaleWhileRevalidate is the window during which stale metadata may be served
while revalidating in the background. Defaults to 168h.

mirror.description:
Mirror configures eager multi-platform pre-seeding via `atmos terraform cache
mirror` (which wraps `terraform/tofu providers mirror`). When omitted, mirror
defaults to the current host platform.

TerraformCacheConfig.description:
TerraformCacheConfig configures the Terraform registry cache. The cache is
execution-environment (runner) configuration — where/how a machine caches
registry content — not stack data.

enabled.description:
Enabled expresses fleet pre-seeding policy; the `cache mirror` command runs
regardless of this value. Defaults to false.

TerraformCacheMirror.description:
TerraformCacheMirror configures eager, multi-platform pre-seeding of the cache
using `terraform/tofu providers mirror`. The mirror writes the canonical
filesystem_mirror layout the lazy proxy already serves, so the same cache
directory works lazily (proxy), eagerly (mirror), and offline (filesystem_mirror).

The target platforms live at components.terraform.platforms (a project-level
property shared with the automatic post-init `providers lock`), not here.

enabled.description: Enabled turns on rendering of the CLI configuration. Defaults to false.

TerraformRCConfig.description:
TerraformRCConfig is a near-opaque passthrough rendered into Terraform's native
CLI configuration (HCL). Only `enabled` is typed; all remaining keys are captured
verbatim in Config and rendered as CLI-config directives (provider_installation,
host, credentials, plugin_cache_dir, ...), so new directives require no Atmos
schema change.

Toolchain.description: Toolchain configures the built-in CLI toolchain management system for installing and managing external tools.

ToolchainProxy.description:
ToolchainProxy exposes a toolchain binary under a command name. The tool is
resolved through Toolchain.Aliases and args are prepended to user arguments.

ref.description: Git ref (tag, branch, or commit) to pin registry version.

ToolchainRegistry.description: ToolchainRegistry defines a registry source for tool metadata.

ToolchainVerification.description: ToolchainVerification configures package integrity and signature checks.

components.description:
Components configures the "atmos list components" command output.
Shows unique component definitions (deduplicated across stacks).

instances.description:
Instances configures the "atmos list instances" command output.
Shows component instances (one entry per component+stack pair).

stacks.description: Stacks configures the "atmos list stacks" command output.

error_mode.description:
ErrorMode is the project-wide default for the `list` commands' (`list stacks`,
`list components`, `list settings`, `list affected`) `--error-mode` flag: how to handle
a recoverable per-value YAML function error (e.g. a Terraform backend that has not been
provisioned yet). Values: "strict", "warn" (default), "silent". An explicit --error-mode
flag or ATMOS_LIST_ERROR_MODE env var overrides this.

TopLevelListConfig.description:
TopLevelListConfig holds command-specific list configurations.
This is a top-level configuration that separates settings for different list commands.

format.description:
Format is the project-wide default for human validation output. Individual
validators may provide a more specific setting (for example
validate.editorconfig.format).

base_path.description:
Path to vendor configuration file or directory containing vendor files.
If a directory is specified, all .yaml files in the directory will be processed in lexicographical order.

list.description: List configuration for vendor list output.

retry.description: Retry configuration for vendor operations (global default).

use.description:
Use specifies the exact Atmos version to use for this project.
When set, Atmos will automatically re-execute itself with the specified version,
installing it first if not already present.

Examples:
  "1.160.0"  - Use exact version
  "latest"   - Resolve and use the latest available version

The re-exec uses the toolchain installer, storing versions in ~/.atmos/bin/cloudposse/atmos/{version}/.

track.description: Track is the default managed version track.

lock_file.description:
LockFile is the path to the managed versions lock file. Relative paths are
resolved from the Atmos base path.

dependencies.description:
Dependencies is the base catalog of external dependencies managed by the
Version Tracker. Track-level dependencies override these entries.

files.description:
Files declares which project files the file managers maintain. When
empty, managers with default paths (github-actions, template) run over
those defaults.

Version.description: Version configures version checking, constraint validation, self-management, and managed external versions.

VersionCheck.description: VersionCheck configures automatic version checking against GitHub releases.

require.description:
Require specifies the semver constraint(s) for Atmos version as a single string.
Multiple constraints are comma-separated and treated as logical AND.
Uses hashicorp/go-version library syntax (same as Terraform).

Examples:
  ">=1.2.3"                    - Minimum version
  "<2.0.0"                     - Maximum version (exclude)
  ">=1.2.3, <2.0.0"            - Range (AND logic)
  ">=2.5.0, !=2.7.0, <3.0.0"   - Complex (multiple AND constraints)
  "~>1.2"                      - Pessimistic constraint (>=1.2.0, <1.3.0)
  "~>1.2.3"                    - Pessimistic constraint (>=1.2.3, <1.3.0)
  "1.2.3"                      - Exact version

enforcement.description:
Enforcement specifies the behavior when version constraint is not satisfied.
Values:
  "fatal" - Exit immediately with error code 1 (default)
  "warn"  - Log warning but continue execution
  "silent" - Skip validation entirely (for debugging)

message.description:
Message provides a custom message to display when constraint fails.
If empty, a default message is shown.

VersionConstraint.description:
VersionConstraint configures version constraint validation for Atmos.
When specified, Atmos validates that the current version satisfies the constraint
before executing any command.

VersionEntry.description: VersionEntry configures one externally managed version.

manager.description: Manager is the file manager name (github-actions, marker, template).

paths.description: Paths are glob patterns (doublestar) relative to the project root.

VersionFileRule.description:
VersionFileRule maps a file manager to the paths it maintains, so a single
`atmos version track apply` sweeps every version-managed file.

VersionGroup.description: VersionGroup configures a Dependabot/Renovate-style batch of updates.

VersionPolicy.description: VersionPolicy configures shared defaults for managed versions.

insecure.description:
Insecure allows plain-HTTP access to the registry (local/emulated
registries only).

VersionProvider.description: VersionProvider configures a concrete backend used to discover versions.

VersionTrack.description: VersionTrack configures a named version lane such as dev, staging, or prod.

pin.description:
Pin selects the artifact form emitted for the entry: "digest" (alias
"sha") locks and renders the immutable identifier (git commit SHA or
OCI sha256 digest) alongside the human-readable version; "none" or
empty keeps plain version references. Strategy decides how far updates
may advance; Pin decides which form is written.

VersionUpdatePolicy.description: VersionUpdatePolicy configures update intent for managed versions.

WorkflowContainer.description:
WorkflowContainer configures a workflow-level container-backed sandbox or a
step-level container override. A YAML value of `false` disables inheritance.

script.description: Inline script body for type: script.

anyOf.description: Predicate (always, never, ci, local, success, failure) or CEL expression.

interactive.description: Interactive attaches host stdin to the step and lets the step handle Ctrl-C (like docker -i).

tty.description: Tty allocates a pseudo-terminal for the step (like docker -t). Combine with interactive for full terminal sessions.

source.description: Source: workdir provisioning (string or source map), or the directory/file to archive (archive step type, string only).

reset.description: Reset the target path before provisioning.

border.description: Border style: none, hidden, normal, rounded, thick, double.

padding.description: Padding: "1" or "1 2" or "1 2 1 2" (top, right, bottom, left).

margin.description: Margin: "1" or "1 2" or "1 2 1 2" (top, right, bottom, left).

url.description: HTTP step type fields (type: http; also accepts the alias type: webhook).

method.description: HTTP method/verb: GET (default), POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS.

body.description: Raw request body (supports templates); mutually exclusive with form.

form.description: Form/JSON body params; mutually exclusive with body.

mode.description: Cast step and session action fields.

jitter.description: Deterministic typing delay variance for simulated typed cast steps.

cursor.description: Show a simulated cursor for cast simulate steps.

action.description:
Container step fields.

Action selects the container verb; the action's parameters are supplied under
the single `with:` key (GitHub Actions `uses`/`with` style). Build/Push/Run/Inspect
are populated from `with:` by UnmarshalYAML based on Action, so they carry no YAML key.

Only cross-cutting execution modifiers stay top-level (provider, runtime_auto_start,
runtime, container). All action parameters — image, command, ports, mounts,
healthcheck, etc. — live under `with:` (decoded into Build/Run/Push/Inspect).

runtime.description: Inline per-step runtime block (e.g. runtime.host for Docker-out-of-Docker).

provider.description: auto, docker, podman, or empty for auto-detect.

container.description: Workflow container override or false to run on host.

ephemeral.description: Run the emulator without persistence for this step (emulator step type).

format.description:
Archive step fields (type: archive). Action reuses the container step's
Action field (create | extract | update | replace); Source reuses the
workdir step's Source field (archive requires it to be a string path).

destination.description: Pack: archive file to write. Extract: directory to extract into.

subpath.description: Pack: nest source content under this path inside the archive. Extract: only extract this path, prefix stripped.

exclude.description: Glob(s); drop matching files, evaluated before include.

mtime.description:
Mtime controls the modification-time metadata stamped into each archive entry
(not the source files on disk, not the archive file's own OS-level mtime):
filesystem (default, same as omitting the field) | epoch (one shared timestamp
for the whole archive) | git (per-entry timestamps). epoch/git also normalize
permission bits. See docs/prd/archive-step.md.

tools.description:
Require step type fields (type: require; also accepts the alias type: assert).
The step is a read-only preconditions gate: it never mutates PATH or the environment.

hint.description: Extra remediation note appended to the failure error (supports templates).

outputs.description: Outputs declares named outputs derived from the step result.

show.description: Show configuration for this step (overrides workflow-level show settings).

WorkflowStep.description: WorkflowStep represents a single step in a workflow.

prefix_separator.description:
PrefixSeparator controls how '/' in component names is handled when
auto-generating backend key prefixes (workspace_key_prefix for S3,
prefix for GCS, key for Azure).

"-" (default): Replace '/' with '-' → services/consul becomes services-consul.
"/": Preserve '/' as-is → services/consul stays services/consul.

yamlFunction.description: Atmos YAML function invocation whose result replaces this value when atmos.yaml is loaded (e.g. `!include`, `!env`, `!exec`).

anyOf.description: Path or URL of the schema resource.

list.description: List holds command-specific list configurations (list.components, list.instances, list.stacks).

title: JSON Schema for the Atmos CLI configuration (atmos.yaml). Version 1.0. https://atmos.tools
description: Configuration for the Atmos CLI, authored in atmos.yaml (or atmos.d fragments). Generated from the Go configuration structs, so it always matches what Atmos actually reads.
